title: asplenium trichomanes subsp. quadrivalens
intro: Asplenium trichomanes subsp. quadrivalens, commonly known as the maidenhair spleenwort, is a subspecies of fern native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids content. The health benefits of Asplenium trichomanes subsp. quadrivalens include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing, due to its astringent and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in water or alcohol to create teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
